title: AI Chip Bottleneck Stack
purpose: Map the obvious AI chip companies to the less obvious public-market bottlenecks that make them possible.
method: Supply-chain-first research model. Start with frontier AI accelerators, then follow foundry, packaging, memory, EDA, equipment, substrates, materials, wafers, gases, and OSAT dependencies downward and sideways.
source_policy: Use official filings and annual reports when available; use industry research for non-disclosed dependency weights. Confidence: high = directly disclosed, medium = supported by filings/industry reports, low = inferred ecosystem logic.
canonical_thesis: For AI chips, the market sees NVIDIA first. The bottleneck stack is TSMC advanced nodes and CoWoS, HBM supply, ABF substrates, EDA software, EUV lithography, process control/metrology, deposition/etch, specialty gases/chemicals, silicon wafers, and advanced packaging/test.

companies.name: SK hynix
companies.public_market_expression: Direct HBM bottleneck; liquid Korea listing.
companies.plain_english_description: Manufactures DRAM, NAND, server memory, and high-bandwidth memory used beside GPUs/AI accelerators.
companies.why_it_matters_now: HBM revenue more than doubled in 2025 and AI demand shifted memory from commodity cycle to specialized infrastructure bottleneck.
companies.bottleneck_notes: HBM was one of the clearest AI chip constraints. SK hynix has leadership and deep NVIDIA exposure.

companies.name: Ajinomoto
companies.public_market_expression: Weird hidden bottleneck: MSG/food conglomerate with ABF film monopoly through Ajinomoto Fine-Techno.
companies.plain_english_description: Consumer food and biotech company whose subsidiary makes Ajinomoto Build-up Film, the insulating dielectric film used in advanced CPU/GPU substrates.
companies.where_in_stack: ABF material supplier upstream of substrate makers.
companies.why_it_matters_now: AI accelerators use larger, higher-layer-count substrates. ABF film demand rises faster than chip units as packages grow and layer counts rise.
companies.bottleneck_notes: Small segment inside a conglomerate, but structurally critical. Downstream substrate makers depend on ABF allocation.
risk_flags: small revenue contribution inside large food company

companies.name: DISCO
companies.public_market_expression: Dicing/grinding/thinning near-monopoly for HBM and advanced packaging.
companies.plain_english_description: Makes precision dicing saws, grinders, polishers and consumables used to cut, thin and polish wafers and packages.
companies.where_in_stack: Wafer thinning / dicing / advanced packaging process equipment.
companies.why_it_matters_now: HBM and 2.5D/3D AI packages require more thinning, grinding and dicing steps. DISCO reported generative-AI-driven shipments and advanced packaging tailwinds.
companies.bottleneck_notes: Razor-and-blade consumables plus high share in critical back-end process steps. Very Jamie Simons: obscure, measurable, recurring, and tied to HBM layer-count intensity.

companies.name: Advantest
companies.public_market_expression: Direct semiconductor test bottleneck for AI accelerators, HBM and high-performance SoCs.
companies.plain_english_description: Makes automated test equipment for SoCs and memory. Customers use its systems to validate AI accelerators, HBM, advanced logic, and high-performance DRAM before shipment.
companies.where_in_stack: Semiconductor test equipment / final yield gate.
companies.why_it_matters_now: AI chips are bigger, hotter, multi-die, HBM-heavy and harder to validate. Advantest says AI/HPC complexity increased the need for test and drove record sales/profit.
companies.bottleneck_notes: Testing becomes a choke point when every advanced package contains multiple chiplets and HBM stacks. Advantest reported CY2025 SoC tester share near 66% in source materials.
risk_flags: customer concentration in AI accelerator leaders

companies.name: Lasertec
companies.public_market_expression: EUV actinic mask inspection monopoly; one of the cleanest weird bottlenecks in semicap.
companies.plain_english_description: Makes actinic EUV mask inspection systems that detect printable photomask defects using EUV wavelength light.
companies.why_it_matters_now: 3nm, 2nm, High-NA EUV and HBM-related advanced nodes raise mask defect sensitivity. Sources describe Lasertec as the only commercial actinic EUV mask inspection provider.
companies.bottleneck_notes: A true scarce capability: if EUV masks cannot be qualified, advanced-node production yield suffers. Stock had a cycle/order trough, making this one of the more interesting contrarian bottlenecks.
risk_flags: customer concentration at TSMC/Samsung/Intel and mask shops

companies.name: ASML Holding
companies.public_market_expression: Direct lithography monopoly; expensive but structurally irreplaceable.
companies.plain_english_description: Makes EUV and DUV lithography tools used by advanced fabs to print tiny chip features.
companies.where_in_stack: Lithography equipment supplier to foundries and memory fabs.
companies.why_it_matters_now: Advanced AI logic and DRAM scaling need EUV. ASML states it is the world's only EUV lithography manufacturer and expects EUV revenue growth from advanced Logic and DRAM.
companies.bottleneck_notes: True monopoly at EUV. No high-end AI chip stack scales without ASML tools, but stock often already prices quality.

companies.name: KLA
companies.public_market_expression: Metrology/process-control bottleneck; direct beneficiary of advanced-node and advanced-packaging complexity.
companies.plain_english_description: Sells inspection, metrology, process control, and yield-management systems used to find defects and improve yield in semiconductor manufacturing.
companies.why_it_matters_now: AI increases chip complexity, wafer value, and packaging complexity. KLA reported strong FY25 growth and advanced packaging process-control revenue exceeding $925M.
companies.bottleneck_notes: Yield control becomes more valuable as wafers/packages get more expensive. Hard to replicate physics/data/software moat.

companies.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company
companies.public_market_expression: Direct foundry and advanced packaging bottleneck.
companies.plain_english_description: Manufactures advanced logic chips for fabless designers and provides advanced packaging/3D stacking technologies including CoWoS, InFO, SoIC, and CPO-related platforms.
companies.why_it_matters_now: AI accelerators require leading-edge nodes and high-end 2.5D packaging. TSMC's annual report states robust AI demand supports leading-edge, specialty, and advanced packaging demand.
companies.bottleneck_notes: The cleanest public bottleneck: advanced-node share plus CoWoS packaging allocation. Taiwan geography creates geopolitical choke point.

companies.name: Unimicron
companies.public_market_expression: Taiwan ABF substrate proxy; less obvious CoWoS/AI package beneficiary.
companies.plain_english_description: Manufactures IC substrates and printed circuit boards, including ABF substrates used in high-performance processors and AI packages.
companies.why_it_matters_now: High-layer-count AI GPU and ASIC substrates are supply constrained and tied to Taiwan's foundry/packaging ecosystem.
companies.bottleneck_notes: Major substrate supplier, but depends on ABF film and Taiwan geography.

companies.name: Ibiden
companies.public_market_expression: Direct high-end ABF substrate public proxy.
companies.plain_english_description: Produces high-performance IC package substrates used under CPUs, GPUs, AI ASICs, and switching chips.
companies.where_in_stack: ABF substrate manufacturer downstream of Ajinomoto and upstream of advanced packaging/foundry flows.
companies.why_it_matters_now: Ibiden expects AI GPU and AI ASIC substrates to drive demand, with large/layered packages exceeding industry supply capacity.
companies.bottleneck_notes: Substrate capacity is expensive and slow to qualify. Ibiden is a high-end supplier, but still depends on Ajinomoto ABF film.

companies.name: Shin-Etsu Chemical
companies.public_market_expression: Silicon wafers + EUV/photoresist/materials exposure in one Japan chemical champion.
companies.plain_english_description: Makes semiconductor silicon wafers and specialty chemicals/materials used by leading-edge fabs.
companies.why_it_matters_now: 300mm wafers for advanced logic and HBM remain a foundational layer; EUV/materials supplier base is narrow.
companies.bottleneck_notes: Boring but powerful: wafer oligopoly plus materials. Better as basket leg than single-name moonshot.

companies.name: HOYA
companies.public_market_expression: EUV mask blank leader hidden inside optics/healthcare conglomerate.
companies.plain_english_description: Produces EUV and DUV mask blanks: the ultra-flat multilayer master substrates used to make photomasks for advanced lithography.
companies.why_it_matters_now: HOYA says demand for mask blanks is supported by AI, cloud, 5G and 3nm/advanced EUV miniaturization; it is developing High-NA and angstrom-generation blanks.
companies.bottleneck_notes: Not as pure as Lasertec, but mask blanks are a narrow supplier base upstream of every EUV photomask.

companies.name: Tokyo Electron
companies.public_market_expression: Japan WFE major across coater/developer, etch, deposition and cleaning.
companies.plain_english_description: Makes semiconductor production equipment used across lithography tracks, etch, deposition, cleaning and inspection-related processes.
companies.why_it_matters_now: AI-driven leading-edge logic and HBM capex pulls forward WFE demand; TEL is a core Japan equipment leg alongside ASML/KLA/Lam/Applied.
companies.bottleneck_notes: Not a single-product monopoly, but essential WFE breadth with Japan supply-chain leverage.

companies.name: Applied Materials
companies.public_market_expression: Broadest semiconductor equipment/tooling proxy.
companies.plain_english_description: Sells materials engineering equipment for deposition, etch, ion implant, CMP, inspection, and advanced packaging.
companies.why_it_matters_now: AI drives foundry logic, DRAM, and advanced packaging spend, the three areas Applied says account for most incremental WFE.
companies.bottleneck_notes: Broad exposure, less pure than ASML/KLA but deeply embedded across manufacturing inflections.

companies.name: BE Semiconductor Industries
companies.public_market_expression: Hybrid bonding / advanced assembly equipment pure play.
companies.plain_english_description: Makes semiconductor assembly equipment, including die attach, thermo-compression and hybrid bonding systems for advanced packaging.
companies.why_it_matters_now: Hybrid bonding enables denser chiplet and 3D architectures with direct copper interconnects. Besi says hybrid bonding is central to generative AI device assembly.
companies.bottleneck_notes: High-upside packaging inflection: more speculative than ASML/KLA but cleaner exposure to next-gen chiplet bonding.

companies.name: Cadence Design Systems
companies.public_market_expression: EDA/software dependency and hyperscaler custom-silicon enabler.
companies.plain_english_description: Provides computational software, EDA, hardware emulation, IP, and AI-driven design tools used to design and verify chips and electronic systems.
companies.why_it_matters_now: Systems companies and hyperscalers are increasingly designing ASICs; Cadence disclosed customers include semiconductor and systems companies and integrates agentic/generative AI in design flows.
companies.bottleneck_notes: One of two dominant EDA vendors; benefits from custom AI ASIC proliferation.
risk_flags: AI tool cannibalization vs uplift uncertainty

companies.name: Entegris
companies.public_market_expression: Advanced materials, filtration, purification, and wafer-handling proxy.
companies.plain_english_description: Supplies materials, filtration, purification, fluid handling, and contamination-control products to semiconductor manufacturing.
companies.why_it_matters_now: EUV/high-NA scaling and metal-oxide resists require ultra-clean precursors, filtration, handling, and delivery systems.
companies.bottleneck_notes: Contamination-control bottleneck: low-profile but essential as defect tolerances tighten.

companies.name: Lam Research
companies.public_market_expression: Deposition/etch and advanced packaging equipment proxy.
companies.plain_english_description: Sells etch, deposition, plating, and related wafer-processing equipment used in logic, memory, and advanced packaging.
companies.why_it_matters_now: Lam says AI is driving new device architectures and packaging approaches that are increasingly deposition- and etch-intensive.
companies.bottleneck_notes: Not monopoly like ASML, but core process steps become more intensive with GAA, backside power, DRAM, NAND, and packaging.

companies.name: Micron Technology
companies.public_market_expression: Liquid U.S. HBM/server memory proxy.
companies.plain_english_description: Manufactures DRAM, NAND, and HBM used in AI servers and accelerators.
companies.why_it_matters_now: NVIDIA's 10-K explicitly names Micron as one of its memory suppliers. HBM supply tightness lifts AI memory economics.
companies.bottleneck_notes: HBM is a true bottleneck, but Micron trails SK hynix in leading HBM share.

companies.name: NVIDIA
companies.public_market_expression: Direct AI accelerator leader; better used as the obvious demand source than the hidden bottleneck.
companies.plain_english_description: Designs GPUs, networking, systems, and AI software stacks. Sells AI accelerators and data-center-scale platforms to hyperscalers, cloud providers, enterprises, and sovereign AI buyers.
companies.why_it_matters_now: NVIDIA's Data Center platform is the largest demand engine for advanced-node wafers, CoWoS packaging, HBM, substrates, EDA, assembly/test, and data-center power.
companies.bottleneck_notes: NVIDIA controls demand and architecture, but depends on bottlenecks it does not fully own: TSMC, CoWoS, HBM, ABF substrates, and assembly/test.

companies.name: Synopsys
companies.public_market_expression: EDA/software dependency for every advanced AI chip design.
companies.plain_english_description: Provides electronic design automation software, semiconductor IP, verification, simulation, and now broader multiphysics/design tools after Ansys.
companies.why_it_matters_now: Advanced AI chips and hyperscaler custom ASICs raise design complexity, verification demand, IP needs, and tool spend.
companies.bottleneck_notes: Design cannot tape out without EDA, but customers often use multiple vendors and switching happens over long cycles.

companies.name: VAT Group
companies.public_market_expression: Vacuum valve hidden bottleneck for semicap tools and vacuum-intensive leading-edge processes.
companies.plain_english_description: Makes high-end vacuum valves, modules and adjacencies used inside semiconductor manufacturing tools.
companies.where_in_stack: Vacuum subsystem supplier to WFE tools.
companies.why_it_matters_now: Leading-edge logic, GAA, HBM and advanced memory are vacuum-intensive. VAT says AI-led semicap ramp supports demand and claims >70% high-end vacuum valve share.
companies.bottleneck_notes: Supplier-of-suppliers play: not bought by NVIDIA, but embedded inside the tools that TSMC/Samsung/Intel/SK hynix need.

companies.name: SUMCO
companies.public_market_expression: Pure silicon wafer oligopoly proxy.
companies.plain_english_description: Supplies silicon wafers used as the starting substrate for semiconductor manufacturing.
companies.why_it_matters_now: SEMI says 300mm wafers for AI-driven logic and HBM are strong even while mature-node recovery is slower.
companies.bottleneck_notes: Important but more cyclical/commodity-like than ABF, HBM, EUV or test.

companies.name: ASE Technology
companies.public_market_expression: Largest OSAT / advanced packaging beneficiary if TSMC outsources more CoWoS-adjacent volume.
companies.plain_english_description: Provides outsourced semiconductor assembly, test, and advanced packaging services.
companies.why_it_matters_now: Industry reports indicate ASE may absorb lower-end/outsourced advanced packaging as TSMC preserves internal high-end capacity.
companies.bottleneck_notes: Biggest OSAT scale, but still downstream of TSMC's proprietary CoWoS choke point.

companies.name: GlobalWafers
companies.public_market_expression: Taiwan silicon wafer proxy; Yahoo symbol corrected to 6488.TWO.
companies.plain_english_description: Manufactures semiconductor wafers for logic, memory and specialty semiconductor applications.
companies.why_it_matters_now: Advanced logic/cloud/HBM wafer demand supports the wafer oligopoly; Taiwan location ties it closely to the foundry ecosystem.
companies.bottleneck_notes: Useful regional wafer proxy, but not the highest-alpha bottleneck versus Ajinomoto/Lasertec/DISCO/Advantest.

companies.name: Air Liquide
companies.public_market_expression: Specialty gases / ultra-high-purity gases / precursors proxy.
companies.plain_english_description: Supplies industrial and specialty gases, ultra-high-purity gases, and advanced materials to semiconductor fabs.
companies.why_it_matters_now: Air Liquide's 2025 reports cite semiconductor gas contracts, ultra-pure gas plants, neon/argon, and molybdenum-based precursors for next-generation semiconductors.
companies.bottleneck_notes: Hidden enabler, but semiconductor exposure is diluted inside a broad industrial gas company.

companies.name: Amkor Technology
companies.public_market_expression: U.S.-listed OSAT/advanced packaging proxy.
companies.plain_english_description: Provides outsourced semiconductor assembly, advanced packaging, wafer-level processing, and test services.
companies.why_it_matters_now: Advanced packaging is no longer back-end commodity work; it is a bottleneck for AI accelerator yield, integration, and throughput.
companies.bottleneck_notes: Useful liquid U.S. proxy, but high-end CoWoS remains TSMC-led. Amkor benefits if advanced packaging outsourcing grows.

companies.name: Siltronic
companies.public_market_expression: Silicon wafer bottleneck proxy.
companies.plain_english_description: Produces polished, epitaxial, and specialty silicon wafers used as the starting substrate for chips.
companies.why_it_matters_now: SEMI says advanced logic and HBM support strong 300mm wafer demand; Siltronic states AI raises silicon demand per application.
companies.bottleneck_notes: Oligopoly layer; not as acute as CoWoS/HBM but foundational and geopolitically relevant.

sankey_edges.notes: NVIDIA 10-K names TSMC and Samsung as wafer foundries; TSMC is dominant for advanced AI accelerators.
Named as NVIDIA foundry and memory supplier, but less central than TSMC for leading AI GPUs.
NVIDIA 10-K names SK hynix as a memory supplier; HBM is a primary AI chip bottleneck.
Named NVIDIA memory supplier; liquid U.S. HBM/server memory proxy.
Named as NVIDIA memory supplier; HBM catch-up competitor.
NVIDIA explicitly says it utilizes CoWoS technology for semiconductor packaging.
High-end AMD AI accelerators compete for advanced packaging/HBM capacity.
Broadcom custom AI ASIC programs for hyperscalers are a major CoWoS demand source.
Hyperscaler custom silicon is a major AI ASIC demand vector; Broadcom is a public custom silicon proxy.
ASML is the only EUV lithography supplier; advanced logic/DRAM scaling depends on EUV.
Process control and metrology become more critical as advanced-node and advanced-packaging yield complexity rises.
Broad materials engineering exposure across foundry logic, DRAM, and advanced packaging.
Deposition/etch intensity rises with GAA, backside power, DRAM scaling, and advanced packaging.
Major Japan WFE supplier; include for semicap basket even though this v1 model did not source TEL filings directly.
ABF film is the weird hidden bottleneck beneath advanced CPU/GPU substrates.
Ibiden is a high-end ABF substrate maker; downstream capacity depends on ABF film allocation.
Unimicron is a major ABF substrate supplier tied to AI package demand.
High-density substrate exposure; liquidity/corporate actions should be checked before trading.
High-performance substrates feed AI GPU/ASIC packaging flows.
Taiwan substrate ecosystem ties into foundry/OSAT advanced packaging flows.
ASE may absorb outsourced/less complex advanced packaging as TSMC capacity remains tight.
Amkor is a liquid U.S.-listed OSAT/advanced packaging proxy.
NVIDIA names Hon Hai as assembly/test/packaging final-products partner.
NVIDIA names Wistron as assembly/test/packaging final-products partner.
NVIDIA names Fabrinet as assembly/test/packaging final-products partner; also optical manufacturing adjacency.
Advanced chip design requires EDA; Synopsys is one of the dominant vendors.
Advanced chip design and systems-company ASICs require EDA and verification tooling.
Systems companies are a fast-growing EDA customer category.
Custom ASIC proliferation expands EDA demand beyond traditional semiconductor companies.
300mm advanced wafers are foundational for AI logic and HBM; oligopoly includes Shin-Etsu, SUMCO, GlobalWafers, Siltronic, SK Siltron.
Leading wafer oligopoly member and also semiconductor materials exposure.
SEMI quote from SUMCO executive highlights strong 300mm demand in advanced AI-driven logic/HBM.
Taiwan wafer supplier with advanced logic/cloud/HBM demand linkage. Yahoo uses 6488.TWO for GlobalWafers.
Siltronic produces wafers used in AI, servers, data centers, and network technology.
Carrier gases, neon, argon, oxygen, nitrogen, and specialty gases are required for lithography, etch, deposition, and fab operations.
2025 Air Liquide report cites semiconductor ultra-pure gas investments and long-term supply contracts.
EUV-qualified materials supplier base is small; qualification cycles are long.
Entegris/JSR-Inpria cross-license spans MOR precursors, filtration, handling, and delivery systems for EUV.
JSR is private after buyout; relevant as non-tradable but important ecosystem bottleneck.
Private optical subsystem dependency; key hidden bottleneck but no direct liquid public expression.
Private laser subsystem dependency inside EUV source chain; important but not directly investable.
High-NA / advanced-node foundry ambitions depend on ASML; Intel is a higher-risk foundry turnaround proxy.
AI/HPC complexity and HBM drive test intensity; Advantest estimates 66% SoC tester share.
HBM and high-performance DRAM expand memory test demand.
Actinic EUV mask inspection gatekeeper for advanced logic and memory masks.
Customers include leading EUV fabs and mask blank manufacturers; order trough may be opportunity.
HOYA produces EUV/DUV mask blanks and is developing High-NA/angstrom-generation products.
Mask blank manufacturers are Lasertec customer ecosystem participants.
HBM and 2.5D packages create grinding/dicing opportunities at multiple process steps.
Generative AI and advanced packaging boosted equipment shipments.
Hybrid bonding is important for generative AI devices and chiplet architectures.
Hybrid bonding orders increased with AI-related 2.5D and photonics applications.
Vacuum valves embedded in vacuum-intensive semicap tools.
Leading-edge AI logic/HBM requires vacuum-intensive processes and high-end valves.

edge_convention: source_node = upstream supplier/enabler; target_node = downstream consumer.

crosscheck_summary.godel_terminal: CF panels confirmed fresh filing coverage for core U.S./ADR names. Use Godel CF as filing router; use Godel DES/FOCUS or Yahoo for quote sanity.
crosscheck_summary.yfinance: 27/28 original+candidate quote checks succeeded. GlobalWafers corrected from 6488.TW to 6488.TWO.
crosscheck_summary.source_review: Strongest new source-backed additions: Advantest, Lasertec, DISCO, HOYA, BESI, VAT Group, Shin-Etsu, SUMCO, GlobalWafers, Tokyo Electron.

research_gaps.gap: Some edges remain qualitative rather than disclosed revenue/customer exposure.
research_gaps.action: Pull 10-K/annual-report customer concentration and segment revenue per ticker before sizing.

research_gaps.gap: Private bottlenecks Zeiss SMT and Trumpf lasers are non-investable directly.
research_gaps.action: Map public indirect expressions: ASML, Carl Zeiss Meditec is not the right pure proxy; look for suppliers to Zeiss/Trumpf.

research_gaps.gap: Godel quote command opened CF/filing panels in this session, not FOCUS quote panels.
research_gaps.action: Use Godel DES/FOCUS manually for chart/quote setup, and yfinance for automated price table.

research_gaps.gap: International ticker venue hygiene matters (example: GlobalWafers Yahoo symbol 6488.TWO).
research_gaps.action: Maintain a quote_symbol field separate from primary_ticker in next schema iteration.

research_gaps.gap: Chart setup is not yet generated for each ticker.
research_gaps.action: Add yfinance 6mo OHLCV support/resistance/volume shelf calculation next.

opportunity_expansion.name: Lasertec
opportunity_expansion.why: Only commercial actinic EUV mask inspection; cycle trough + monopoly = asymmetric watch.

opportunity_expansion.name: DISCO
opportunity_expansion.why: HBM layer-count intensity multiplies wafer thinning/dicing steps; consumables recurring revenue.

opportunity_expansion.name: Advantest
opportunity_expansion.why: AI multi-die/HBM complexity drives test intensity; source cites record results and share gains.

opportunity_expansion.name: HOYA
opportunity_expansion.why: EUV mask blanks are narrow-base upstream material bottleneck for advanced photomasks.

opportunity_expansion.name: VAT Group
opportunity_expansion.why: Supplier-of-suppliers: high-end vacuum valves inside the tools used for AI logic/HBM.

opportunity_expansion.name: BESI
opportunity_expansion.why: Hybrid bonding pure play for future 3D/chiplet/HBM packaging.
